name: Enid's Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Butter a 10x15-inch baking sheet (jellyroll pan). Dust with flour and shake out excess.
3. Melt the chocolate and butter in a small saucepan over very low heat, or in the top of a double boiler. Set aside to cool slightly.
4. Beat the eggs, sugar, and vanilla in a bowl with an electric mixer until thick and fluffy.
5. Stir the melted chocolate mixture into the egg mixture.
6. Add 1 3/4 cups flour and the salt; mix until just combined. Do not overmix.
7. Stir in the chopped walnuts, if desired.
8. Bake in the center of the oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the center of the brownies is just set. Allow to cool completely before cutting into squares about 2x2 inches.
9. Per brownie (without nuts): approximately 212 calories, 13g fat.
